text: Unit tests check small pieces of code, like single functions or methods. They help developers find problems early, making the code easier to debug. Integration tests, on the other hand, verify that different parts of an application work together correctly. For example, they might check if a database and a user interface communicate properly. Test-driven development is a technique where you write a test before writing the actual code. This approach forces clear planning and helps prevent bugs. Together, unit tests, integration tests, and test-driven development increase developer confidence. When changes are made, passing tests reassure the team that the software still works as expected.
